content: Just as classroom vocabulary learning of "reflection" frames the concept as a metacognitive skill for learners to correct their own language errors by re-examining their prior output, the autonomous Reflexion agent framework can borrow this explicit, educational scaffolding to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by grounding abstract self-improvement in step-by-step error-spotting practice similar to how students learn to revise their writing. This reframing turns implicit LLM self-correction into a structured, teachable skill that mirrors human metacognitive learning.
